project.name: vllm
project.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for LLMs, originally developed at UC Berkeley's Sky Computing Lab and offering features such as PagedAttention, continuous batching, prefix caching, optimized attention/GEMM kernels, quantization, speculative decoding, and an OpenAI-compatible API server. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Mandatory contribution policy and development workflow guide for AI-assisted contributions to vLLM, covering duplicate-work checks, environment setup with uv, testing, linting, and commit attribution rules.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Pointer file that delegates Claude Code agent instructions to AGENTS.md.

nodes.name: CMakeLists.txt
nodes.summary: Top-level CMake build configuration for vLLM's C++/CUDA/HIP custom operator extensions, defining target architectures, dependencies, and the _C/_moe_C/_rocm_C/_C_audio extension modules.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-backend (CUDA, ROCm/HIP, CPU) build orchestration spanning 1254 lines and assembling many kernel TUs into Python extensions.

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
nodes.summary: Adopts the Contributor Covenant code of conduct outlining community standards and enforcement guidelines for the vLLM project.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Stub pointer that redirects contributors to the official vLLM contributing documentation site.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project landing page introducing vLLM as a high-throughput, memory-efficient LLM inference and serving engine, with feature highlights, install instructions, and links to docs.

nodes.name: RELEASE.md
nodes.summary: Release process documentation describing version cadence, branching strategy, and the patch/minor/major release workflow for vLLM maintainers.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Security policy describing the responsible disclosure process and the vLLM security advisory channels for reporting vulnerabilities.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Canonical Python project metadata configuring build backend, dependencies, optional extras, ruff/mypy linting, and pytest settings for the vllm package.

nodes.name: setup.py
nodes.summary: Setuptools build script that drives CMake-based compilation of vLLM's CUDA/ROCm/CPU/XPU/TPU extensions, computes the version string, and provides a precompiled-wheel fast path that downloads prebuilt artifacts from S3/PyPI to skip native rebuilds.
nodes.languageNotes: Custom cmake_build_ext and precompiled_build_ext distutils command classes; precompiled_wheel_utils handles cross-platform wheel resolution for CUDA, ROCm, CPU, and XPU variants.

nodes.name: get_vllm_version
nodes.summary: Computes the vLLM version string by combining the static version with device-suffix tags (cu/rocm/cpu/xpu/tpu) and optional local commit metadata.

nodes.name: get_requirements
nodes.summary: Resolves the appropriate requirements file for the active hardware backend (CUDA, ROCm, CPU, XPU, TPU) and returns the parsed dependency list.

nodes.name: get_nvcc_cuda_version
nodes.summary: Invokes nvcc and parses the CUDA toolkit version, used to gate optional kernels and architecture flags.

nodes.name: get_rocm_version
nodes.summary: Detects the installed ROCm version by probing rocm-smi and the ROCm path, used for AMD GPU build configuration.

nodes.name: bundle_tcmalloc
nodes.summary: Copies the tcmalloc shared library into the wheel build directory so users can opt into the higher-throughput allocator without a system-wide install.

nodes.name: cmake_build_ext
nodes.summary: Custom setuptools command that configures and invokes CMake to build vLLM's native extensions, choosing job parallelism, ccache/sccache use, and target architectures based on the host environment.

nodes.name: precompiled_wheel_utils
nodes.summary: Helper class that locates and downloads prebuilt vLLM wheels from PyPI/S3 matching the host CUDA/ROCm variant, then patches the in-tree package with the precompiled native libraries to skip CMake compilation.

nodes.name: use_existing_torch.py
nodes.summary: Helper script that strips torch/torchvision/torchaudio pins from requirements files so a developer's pre-installed PyTorch wheel is reused rather than overwritten during install.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Walks every requirements*.txt file and rewrites lines mentioning torch/torchvision/torchaudio so they no longer pin specific versions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Top-level vllm package entry point that exposes the public API surface (LLM, EngineArgs, SamplingParams, PoolingParams, completion/chat helpers) and re-exports the version string for downstream consumers.

nodes.name: _aiter_ops.py
nodes.summary: Python wrappers around AMD's AITER kernels providing fused-MoE, paged attention, MLA decode, RMSNorm+quant, allreduce-fused-RMSNorm, and W8A8/MXFP4 GEMM ops with custom-op registration and impl/fake variants for torch.compile.
nodes.languageNotes: Each kernel exposes paired _impl and _fake implementations so torch.compile can trace through them; rocm_aiter_ops aggregates capability flags read from environment variables.

nodes.name: rocm_aiter_ops
nodes.summary: Static facade exposing AITER-backed ops (fused MoE, MLA decode, GEMM variants, RMSNorm+quant, allreduce-fused norm, rotary embedding, paged attention) gated by environment-variable feature flags for selective ROCm acceleration.

nodes.name: AiterCustomAllreduceProto
nodes.summary: Protocol describing the AITER custom-allreduce object used for fused allreduce + RMSNorm collectives.

nodes.name: _rocm_aiter_fused_moe_impl
nodes.summary: Concrete fused mixture-of-experts kernel call into AITER, dispatching across activation, quantization, and shared-expert configurations.

nodes.name: _rocm_aiter_mla_decode_fwd_impl
nodes.summary: Performs DeepSeek-style multi-head latent attention decode forward on ROCm via AITER, handling reduction maps and scratch indptrs.

nodes.name: _rocm_aiter_fused_allreduce_rmsnorm_impl
nodes.summary: Fuses NCCL/RCCL allreduce with the following residual+RMSNorm to remove an inter-collective latency hop.

nodes.name: if_aiter_supported
nodes.summary: Decorator that gates a function so it only runs when AITER is detected and supported on the current GPU; otherwise returns a no-op stub.

nodes.name: _custom_ops.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Python facade over vLLM's compiled C++/CUDA custom ops covering paged attention v1/v2, RMSNorm, rotary embedding, AWQ/GPTQ/Marlin quantized GEMMs, FP8/FP4/CUTLASS MoE GEMMs, machete and cutlass-w4a8 mixed-precision kernels.
nodes.languageNotes: 3700+ lines wrapping the native _C, _moe_C, _rocm_C, and _C_audio extensions; each op forwards to torch.ops.<extension>.<op_name>.

nodes.name: paged_attention_v1
nodes.summary: Calls the v1 paged-attention CUDA kernel that consumes block tables and per-sequence lengths to compute decode-step attention against the KV cache.

nodes.name: paged_attention_v2
nodes.summary: Calls the v2 paged-attention CUDA kernel which streams per-block partial sums into final softmax for longer sequences.

nodes.name: paged_attention_rocm
nodes.summary: ROCm-specific paged attention kernel entry point with AMD-tuned MFMA type and FP8 output scaling.

nodes.name: rotary_embedding
nodes.summary: Applies fused rotary positional embedding to query/key tensors using a precomputed cos_sin cache; supports NeoX style and inverse rotation.

nodes.name: fused_add_rms_norm
nodes.summary: In-place residual add followed by RMSNorm, reducing memory traffic in transformer blocks.

nodes.name: cutlass_scaled_mm
nodes.summary: Calls the CUTLASS scaled GEMM kernel for FP8/INT8 quantized matmul with per-tensor or per-token scales and optional bias.

nodes.name: cutlass_moe_mm
nodes.summary: Performs a grouped MoE matmul through CUTLASS with per-expert problem sizes and activation/weight scales, used by FP8 MoE backends.

nodes.name: marlin_gemm
nodes.summary: Marlin INT4/FP8 mixed-precision GEMM with optional zero-points, group scales, and atomic-add reduction for activation-quantized models.

nodes.name: machete_mm
nodes.summary: Calls the Machete mixed-precision GEMM (Hopper tuned) supporting per-channel and per-token scales for AWQ/GPTQ-style models.

nodes.name: apply_repetition_penalties
nodes.summary: Dispatches to the CUDA or torch reference implementation of repetition penalty application during sampling.

nodes.name: rms_norm_dynamic_per_token_quant
nodes.summary: Fuses RMSNorm with dynamic per-token quantization (FP8/INT8) for activation quantization paths.

nodes.name: CPUDNNLGEMMHandler
nodes.summary: Holds the lifetime of an oneDNN GEMM handle for CPU execution, releasing it on garbage collection.

nodes.name: _xpu_ops.py
nodes.summary: Intel XPU backend op wrappers exposing GDN attention, DeepSeek scaling RoPE, MXFP8/MXFP4 quantization, varlen flash-attn, and indexer KV-cache helpers via custom-op registration.

nodes.name: xpu_ops
nodes.summary: Static facade for Intel XPU custom ops: dynamic per-token int8 quant, varlen flash attention, scheduler metadata, indexer KV quantization, and top-k routing kernels.

nodes.name: beam_search.py
nodes.summary: Implements beam-search support data structures (sequences, instances, outputs) and length-penalty scoring used by the LLM offline inference path for beam decoding.

nodes.name: BeamSearchSequence
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing one beam: generated tokens, cumulative logprob, multimodal data, and helpers to materialize encoder/decoder inputs for the next step.

nodes.name: BeamSearchInstance
nodes.summary: Per-request beam-search state holding the active beam list and any completed beams along with config such as length_penalty.

nodes.name: get_beam_search_score
nodes.summary: Computes the length-normalized score for a beam given its cumulative logprob, EOS token, and length penalty.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty init marking vllm.compilation as a Python package; sub-modules expose the torch.compile integration surface.

nodes.name: backends.py
nodes.summary: Implements VllmBackend, the custom torch.compile dynamo backend that splits the FX graph on attention boundaries, schedules per-piece Inductor compilation with caching, and wraps each piecewise sub-graph in a CUDA graph when eligible.
nodes.languageNotes: Coordinates CompilerManager, PiecewiseCompileInterpreter, and CUDAGraphWrapper to produce piecewise-compiled, piecewise-replayed model executors.

nodes.name: VllmBackend
nodes.summary: The dynamo backend invoked by torch.compile that orchestrates graph splitting on attention ops, per-subgraph Inductor compilation, post-graph passes, standalone-compile artifact collection, and CUDA graph wrapping.

nodes.name: CompilerManager
nodes.summary: Owns the underlying CompilerInterface adaptor, manages the on-disk compile cache (load/save), computes hashes including environment factors, and serializes Inductor artifacts for warm reuse.

nodes.name: PiecewiseCompileInterpreter
nodes.summary: FX graph interpreter that walks the split graph and dispatches each submodule to the compiler manager, building the piecewise execution callable.

nodes.name: split_graph
nodes.summary: Splits the dynamo-traced FX graph at every splitting op (attention) into named subgraphs while preserving symbolic-shape metadata, returning the split graph and per-node assignment.

nodes.name: wrap_with_cudagraph_if_needed
nodes.summary: Conditionally wraps a piecewise subgraph in a CUDAGraphWrapper based on the compilation config, producing a static-graph callable for replay.

nodes.name: make_compiler
nodes.summary: Factory that returns the configured CompilerInterface adaptor (Inductor standalone, Inductor in-process, or eager) based on the compilation config.

nodes.name: _decompose_size_nodes
nodes.summary: Rewrites tensor.size() FX nodes into explicit symbolic-shape operations so they survive subgraph splitting without breaking guards.

nodes.name: base_static_graph.py
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for static-graph wrappers (CUDA graphs, Inductor standalone) defining the __call__ contract used by the compilation backend.

nodes.name: AbstractStaticGraphWrapper
nodes.summary: ABC defining the static-graph wrapper interface: __init__ takes the runnable + config and __call__ replays the captured graph for the given inputs.

nodes.name: caching.py
nodes.summary: Implements StandaloneCompiledArtifacts and VllmSerializableFunction which serialize Inductor-compiled FX subgraphs to disk and reconstruct them in-process for warm-start torch.compile reuse.
nodes.languageNotes: Reconstructs SerializableFunction from a mega-artifact (combined graph module + standalone-compile blobs), patching pytree.map for slice handling.

nodes.name: StandaloneCompiledArtifacts
nodes.summary: Container for per-submodule Inductor standalone-compile artifacts supporting insertion, lazy load, size accounting, and pickle round-trips.

nodes.name: VllmSerializableFunction
nodes.summary: Serializable wrapper around a compiled FX graph module plus its standalone-compile artifacts that knows how to (de)serialize the graph and rebind it to runtime fake-mode + symbolic shapes.

nodes.name: reconstruct_serializable_fn_from_mega_artifact
nodes.summary: Rebuilds a VllmSerializableFunction from a combined disk artifact, restoring the graph module, standalone-compile blobs, and symbolic-shape index maps under the requested fake mode.

nodes.name: aot_compile_hash_factors
nodes.summary: Returns the list of environment and config inputs that participate in the cache key for ahead-of-time compiled artifacts.

nodes.name: codegen.py
nodes.summary: Generates Python source for the piecewise execution function that calls each compiled submodule in sequence, then exec's it to produce a fast top-level callable.

nodes.name: generate_execution_code_with_name
nodes.summary: Emits Python source for a function that calls each split submodule in topo order, threading intermediate tensors as named locals.

nodes.name: compile_execution_fn
nodes.summary: Compiles the generated source string into a real Python function bound to each submodule's compiled callable.

nodes.name: compiler_interface.py
nodes.summary: Defines CompilerInterface and three adaptors: InductorAdaptor (in-process compile_fx), InductorStandaloneAdaptor (AOT-serializable artifacts), and EagerAdaptor (no compile fallback) plus shape-env tweaks for cache hit guarantees.

nodes.name: CompilerInterface
nodes.summary: Abstract contract for vLLM compiler adaptors: cache initialization, hash computation, compile, and load operations consumed by CompilerManager.

nodes.name: InductorStandaloneAdaptor
nodes.summary: CompilerInterface implementation that drives torch._inductor.standalone_compile to produce a serializable artifact suitable for the on-disk cache.

nodes.name: InductorAdaptor
nodes.summary: CompilerInterface implementation that calls torch._inductor.compile_fx in-process, manages metrics, and patches Inductor cache directories per piecewise subgraph.

nodes.name: EagerAdaptor
nodes.summary: Trivial CompilerInterface that returns the FX graph unchanged for the no-compile fallback path.

nodes.name: AlwaysHitShapeEnv
nodes.summary: Stub ShapeEnv that always reports guard satisfaction, used to coerce the Inductor cache into hitting after warm restore.

nodes.name: get_inductor_factors
nodes.summary: Returns the Inductor configuration flags that should be incorporated into the compile-cache hash so cache hits are config-correct.

nodes.name: _patch_standalone_compile_atomic_save
nodes.summary: Monkey-patches torch._inductor.standalone_compile to save artifacts atomically so concurrent compiles do not see partial files.

nodes.name: counter.py
nodes.summary: Test/diagnostic singleton CompilationCounter that tracks how many subgraphs were compiled, cache-hit, and CUDA-graph-captured during a run.

nodes.name: CompilationCounter
nodes.summary: Dataclass-style counter capturing per-stage compilation events with a context-manager `expect` helper used by tests to assert counts.

nodes.name: cuda_graph.py
nodes.summary: Implements CUDAGraphWrapper which captures a CUDA graph for each shape bucket on first call and replays it on subsequent calls, plus per-shape stat logging.

nodes.name: CUDAGraphWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps a callable so each invocation matches a captured CUDA graph keyed on input shapes, falling back to capture on miss; supports unwrap and bulk clear.

nodes.name: CUDAGraphLogging
nodes.summary: Collects CUDA-graph capture/replay metrics across shapes and emits a formatted metric table for diagnostics.

nodes.name: decorators.py
nodes.summary: Provides the @support_torch_compile decorator suite that retrofits nn.Module classes with vLLM's torch.compile integration: dynamic-arg dim marking, AOT artifact loading, conditional compile gating, and CUDA-graph partition wrapping.
nodes.languageNotes: Multiple overloaded support_torch_compile signatures pattern-match how callers pass options; the underlying _support_torch_compile is the real implementation.

nodes.name: _support_torch_compile
nodes.summary: Class-decorator implementation that injects __init__/forward overrides marking dynamic dims, loading any AOT-compiled artifact, and dispatching to torch.compile with VllmBackend on first invocation.

nodes.name: _try_load_aot_compiled_fn
nodes.summary: Attempts to load a previously serialized AOT-compiled forward function from the cache directory and rebind it to the live model, skipping recompilation.

nodes.name: _verify_source_unchanged
nodes.summary: Hashes the model source and compares to the artifact's source_info to invalidate cached AOT compiles when code changes.

nodes.name: maybe_use_cudagraph_partition_wrapper
nodes.summary: Decides whether the model should use the CUDA-graph partition wrapper (per-attention-block static graphs) based on the compilation config and decoration metadata.

nodes.name: ignore_torch_compile
nodes.summary: Class decorator that opts a subclass out of @support_torch_compile inheritance, useful when a derived model needs to run eagerly.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Helper routines for the scheduler: stop-condition checking, output sequence repetition detection, and list mutation utilities.

nodes.name: check_sequence_repetition
nodes.summary: Detects pathological repeating-token loops in generation output as an additional stop signal.

nodes.name: remove_all
nodes.summary: In-place removal of a set of items from a list while preserving order; used for batched scheduler state cleanup.

nodes.name: check_stop
nodes.summary: Evaluates EOS, stop tokens, max-tokens, and stop strings to determine whether a request should finish.

nodes.name: single_type_kv_cache_manager.py
nodes.summary: Per-attention-type KV cache managers (full, sliding, chunked, mamba, cross, sink) plugged into the coordinator to handle group-specific cache hit and allocation rules.

nodes.name: SingleTypeKVCacheManager
nodes.summary: Abstract base providing common allocation, caching, and freeing logic for a single KV cache group.

nodes.name: FullAttentionManager
nodes.summary: KV cache manager for standard full attention layers with full prefix-cache support.

nodes.name: SlidingWindowManager
nodes.summary: KV cache manager for sliding-window attention; tracks window-aware prefix hits and drops blocks outside the window.

nodes.name: ChunkedLocalAttentionManager
nodes.summary: KV cache manager for chunked local attention used by Llama 4 and similar architectures.

nodes.name: MambaManager
nodes.summary: Cache manager for Mamba/SSM state tensors; allocates state slots rather than token-block prefixes.

nodes.name: CrossAttentionManager
nodes.summary: Cache manager for encoder-decoder cross-attention KV that does not participate in the prefix cache.

nodes.name: SinkFullAttentionManager
nodes.summary: Variant of FullAttentionManager that pins the initial sink tokens in the cache for streaming-LLM-style inference.

nodes.name: get_manager_for_kv_cache_spec
nodes.summary: Factory that returns the appropriate SingleTypeKVCacheManager subclass for a given KV cache spec.

nodes.name: cudagraph_dispatcher.py
nodes.summary: Maintains and dispatches captured CUDA graphs by batch descriptor (batch size, lora cases, padded shape) to accelerate steady-state inference.

nodes.name: CudagraphDispatcher
nodes.summary: Manages padded batch descriptors and selects the right captured CUDA graph at runtime, fall back-friendly when no graph matches.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Wire-protocol message dataclasses and enums shared between engine processes (requests, outputs, events, reconfiguration).

nodes.name: FinishReason
nodes.summary: Enumeration of reasons a request can finish (stop, length, abort, error) used in EngineCoreOutput.

nodes.name: EngineCoreRequest
nodes.summary: Wire-format request payload sent from the AsyncLLM frontend to the EngineCore process.

nodes.name: EngineCoreEvent
nodes.summary: Lifecycle events (queued, scheduled, preempted) emitted alongside outputs for observability.

nodes.name: EngineCoreOutput
nodes.summary: Per-request output emitted from the engine (new tokens, logprobs, finish reason, events).

nodes.name: EngineCoreOutputs
nodes.summary: Batch wrapper aggregating all per-request EngineCoreOutput entries for a single engine step.

nodes.name: EngineCoreRequestType
nodes.summary: Enum of message types (ADD, ABORT, UTILITY, etc.) used on the engine input queue.

nodes.name: async_llm.py
nodes.summary: AsyncLLM frontend that sits between the OpenAI-compatible API server and the EngineCore process: tokenizes inputs, streams outputs, handles aborts, and exposes async iterators per request.

nodes.name: InputStreamError
nodes.summary: Exception type signaling failure on the input-tokenization side of the engine pipeline.

nodes.name: AsyncLLM
nodes.summary: Top-level async engine entry point: launches EngineCore, accepts add_request calls, and yields EngineCoreOutputs as async iterators back to API consumers.

nodes.name: coordinator.py
nodes.summary: Implements the data-parallel (DP) coordinator process that synchronizes engine state across DP replicas via ZMQ pub/sub messaging, tracking which engines are running, paused, or idle.

nodes.name: DPCoordinator
nodes.summary: Front-end-side coordinator that spawns and communicates with the DP coordinator subprocess to broadcast wave-step state.

nodes.name: DPCoordinatorProc
nodes.summary: Subprocess implementation that aggregates engine status messages and broadcasts pause/resume signals to keep DP replicas wave-synchronized.

nodes.name: core.py
nodes.summary: Defines the EngineCore that runs the scheduler and model executor inner loop, plus background process and Ray actor wrappers used to host the engine in standalone, multiprocess, and DP/Ray deployments.
nodes.languageNotes: Highly polymorphic: same engine logic is wrapped by EngineCoreProc (multiproc), DPEngineCoreProc (data-parallel), and EngineCoreActor (Ray).

nodes.name: EngineCore
nodes.summary: Core inference engine that owns the scheduler, model executor, and KV cache; drives the add_request/step/abort lifecycle each iteration.

nodes.name: EngineCoreProc
nodes.summary: Background process wrapper that runs an EngineCore in its own subprocess and exchanges requests/outputs with the front-end via ZMQ sockets.

nodes.name: DPEngineCoreProc
nodes.summary: Data-parallel-aware engine subprocess that participates in DP wave synchronization with the coordinator and peer replicas.

nodes.name: EngineCoreActorMixin
nodes.summary: Mixin providing common Ray actor behaviors for engine core actor variants used in distributed deployments.

nodes.name: EngineCoreActor
nodes.summary: Ray actor that hosts an EngineCore for cluster-distributed inference deployments.

nodes.name: core_client.py
nodes.summary: Front-end client(s) that talk to one or more EngineCore processes — supports in-process, sync multi-process, async multi-process, and data-parallel load-balanced variants.

nodes.name: EngineCoreClient
nodes.summary: Abstract base client defining the request/abort/profile API used by the LLM engine to talk to its core(s).

nodes.name: InprocClient
nodes.summary: In-process EngineCore client used when the engine runs in the same Python process as the front-end.

nodes.name: MPClient
nodes.summary: Base multi-process client that owns ZMQ sockets, output thread, and lifecycle management for one or more engine subprocesses.

nodes.name: SyncMPClient
nodes.summary: Synchronous multi-process client used by the offline LLMEngine wrapper.

nodes.name: AsyncMPClient
nodes.summary: Asynchronous multi-process client used by the AsyncLLM/server front-end to interact with engine subprocesses via asyncio.

nodes.name: DPAsyncMPClient
nodes.summary: Async client variant that fans requests out across multiple data-parallel engine replicas.

nodes.name: DPLBAsyncMPClient
nodes.summary: Load-balancing async DP client that routes requests across replicas using running-queue length and external LB hints.

nodes.name: BackgroundResources
nodes.summary: Tracks ZMQ sockets, processes, and threads owned by an MP client so they can be cleanly torn down on shutdown.

nodes.name: _process_utility_output
nodes.summary: Resolves a pending utility-RPC future with the response received from an engine core process.

nodes.name: detokenizer.py
nodes.summary: Incremental detokenization for streaming generation, with fast (Rust tokenizer) and slow (HF tokenizer) implementations and stop-string detection.

nodes.name: IncrementalDetokenizer
nodes.summary: Factory dispatching to fast or slow incremental detokenizer based on tokenizer capability and request settings.

nodes.name: BaseIncrementalDetokenizer
nodes.summary: Common detokenizer state and stop-checking logic shared by fast and slow implementations.

nodes.name: FastIncrementalDetokenizer
nodes.summary: Detokenizer using HuggingFace Tokenizers' native streaming decode for low-latency token-by-token output.

nodes.name: SlowIncrementalDetokenizer
nodes.summary: Fallback detokenizer that calls convert_ids_to_tokens incrementally for tokenizers without native streaming.

nodes.name: check_stop_strings
nodes.summary: Scans newly emitted text for any configured stop string and returns truncation info if found.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines engine-level exception types signalling generation errors and dead engine state.

nodes.name: input_processor.py
nodes.summary: Validates and preprocesses incoming generation requests — applies sampling params, tokenizes prompts, and handles multimodal/structured-output preprocessing before dispatch to the engine core.

nodes.name: InputProcessor
nodes.summary: Builds EngineCoreRequest objects from user-facing prompts/params, applying tokenization, multimodal embedding, and lora resolution.

nodes.name: llm_engine.py
nodes.summary: Synchronous v1 LLMEngine facade — composes input processor, engine-core client, and output processor for offline batch inference.

nodes.name: LLMEngine
nodes.summary: Top-level synchronous engine class providing add_request/step methods used by the offline LLM API.

nodes.name: logprobs.py
nodes.summary: Per-request streaming logprob processor that incrementally builds OpenAI-style sample/prompt logprob outputs from token-level data.

nodes.name: LogprobsProcessor
nodes.summary: Accumulates per-step logprob outputs into request-scoped Logprobs/PromptLogprobs structures for streaming and final responses.

nodes.name: output_processor.py
nodes.summary: Converts engine-core token outputs into RequestOutput objects for the user — handles per-request state, detokenization, logprobs, and streaming aggregation.

nodes.name: RequestState
nodes.summary: Per-request state holding detokenizer, logprob processor, and stream queue; updated each engine step until completion.

nodes.name: OutputProcessor
nodes.summary: Top-level processor that maintains RequestState entries and transforms engine-core outputs into user-facing RequestOutput batches.

nodes.name: RequestOutputCollector
nodes.summary: Async queue-like collector buffering RequestOutput chunks for delivery to a single streaming consumer.

nodes.name: parallel_sampling.py
nodes.summary: Implements ParentRequest fan-out for n>1 sampling — splits a parent request into n child requests and aggregates their outputs.

nodes.name: ParentRequest
nodes.summary: Tracks an n-sample parent request and its child requests, merging their outputs into a single RequestOutput.

nodes.name: tensor_ipc.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight zero-copy tensor IPC channel using shared memory or CUDA IPC handles to pass tensors between engine processes.

nodes.name: TensorIpcSender
nodes.summary: Sender side of the tensor IPC channel that exports tensors via shared-memory or CUDA IPC handles.

nodes.name: TensorIpcReceiver
nodes.summary: Receiver side of the tensor IPC channel that materializes incoming handles back into local tensors.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for launching and supervising engine-core processes/actors — defines CoreEngine state, ZMQ address allocation, process managers, and DP/Ray launch orchestration.

nodes.name: CoreEngineProcManager
nodes.summary: Spawns and supervises engine-core subprocesses, propagating signals and handling crash/exit semantics.

nodes.name: CoreEngineActorManager
nodes.summary: Builds Ray placement groups and engine-core actors for distributed and DP deployments, coordinating handshakes and shutdown.

nodes.name: set_device_control_env_var
nodes.summary: Sets the platform-appropriate device-visibility env var (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES, etc.) for an engine subprocess.

nodes.name: get_device_indices
nodes.summary: Computes device index assignments for engine workers based on local rank, world size, and DP layout.

nodes.name: get_engine_zmq_addresses
nodes.summary: Allocates the set of ZMQ socket addresses (input, output, coordinator) used to wire up an engine-core process.

nodes.name: launch_core_engines
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestration that launches engine-core processes/actors based on parallel config (multiproc, Ray, DP) and returns handles to them.

nodes.name: wait_for_engine_startup
nodes.summary: Blocks until all engine-core processes/actors have completed their handshake and are ready to serve requests.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the v1 executor module, re-exporting the abstract Executor base class.

nodes.name: abstract.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract Executor interface that all v1 executor backends (uniproc, multiproc, ray) implement to drive model workers.

nodes.name: Executor
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for v1 executors, providing the worker-facing API for execute_model, profiling, KV-cache init, and lifecycle.

nodes.name: multiproc_executor.py
nodes.summary: Multi-process executor that forks one worker process per tensor-parallel rank, communicating over ZMQ and shared memory.

nodes.name: MultiprocExecutor
nodes.summary: Driver-side executor that owns and orchestrates worker subprocesses, dispatching execute_model calls and aggregating outputs.

nodes.name: WorkerProc
nodes.summary: Worker-side process that hosts a single GPU worker, listens for RPC commands, and runs model execution.

nodes.name: FutureWrapper
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around a concurrent.futures.Future returned by multiproc executor calls.

nodes.name: WorkerProcHandle
nodes.summary: Handle struct holding the process, ZMQ sockets, and metadata for a worker subprocess.

nodes.name: set_multiprocessing_worker_envs
nodes.summary: Configures process-level env vars (threading, NCCL, CUDA visibility) before spawning a worker subprocess.

nodes.name: ray_env_utils.py
nodes.summary: Small helper that captures relevant driver environment variables to forward to Ray worker actors.

nodes.name: get_driver_env_vars
nodes.summary: Returns a dict of driver env vars (e.g., VLLM_*, NCCL_*) to propagate into Ray worker runtime environments.

nodes.name: ray_executor.py
nodes.summary: Legacy Ray-based distributed executor that instantiates one Ray actor per worker rank using placement groups for collocation.

nodes.name: RayDistributedExecutor
nodes.summary: Driver that creates Ray worker actors, dispatches execute_model calls across them, and manages NCCL groups.

nodes.name: ray_executor_v2.py
nodes.summary: New-generation Ray executor that reuses the multiproc WorkerProc protocol over Ray actors for unified worker behavior.

nodes.name: RayExecutorV2
nodes.summary: Driver class for the v2 Ray executor that orchestrates RayWorkerProc actors with placement-group collocation.

nodes.name: RayWorkerProc
nodes.summary: Ray actor wrapping the multiproc WorkerProc loop so distributed workers share the same RPC contract.

nodes.name: ray_utils.py
nodes.summary: Ray-specific helpers used by the Ray executors — placement group creation/verification, actor naming, node sorting, and cluster initialization.

nodes.name: initialize_ray_cluster
nodes.summary: Initializes the Ray runtime if needed and creates or reuses placement groups sized for the configured world.

nodes.name: _verify_bundles
nodes.summary: Validates that placement-group bundles match expected resource shape (GPUs/TPUs per bundle).

nodes.name: get_bundles_sorted_by_node
nodes.summary: Sorts placement-group bundles by node so the driver bundle is first and intra-node ranks are colocated.

nodes.name: _wait_until_pg_ready
nodes.summary: Polls a Ray placement group until it is fully scheduled, surfacing helpful diagnostics on timeout.

nodes.name: _wait_until_pg_removed
nodes.summary: Waits until a Ray placement group has been fully torn down before returning.

nodes.name: detach_zero_copy_from_model_runner_output
nodes.summary: Detaches zero-copy tensor references from a model-runner output so it can be safely sent across Ray actor boundaries.

nodes.name: get_bundles_for_indices
nodes.summary: Selects a subset of placement-group bundles by index for partial assignments (e.g., DP slices).

nodes.name: build_actor_name
nodes.summary: Constructs a stable, descriptive Ray actor name from rank and run identifiers.

nodes.name: get_num_nodes_in_placement_group
nodes.summary: Returns the count of distinct physical nodes spanned by a Ray placement group.

nodes.name: uniproc_executor.py
nodes.summary: Single-process executor for non-parallel deployments — runs the worker in-thread and supports an external-launcher (torchrun) variant.

nodes.name: UniProcExecutor
nodes.summary: Executor that creates a single in-process worker and runs all execute_model calls synchronously.

nodes.name: ExecutorWithExternalLauncher
nodes.summary: Executor variant assuming an external launcher (torchrun, mpirun) handles process spawning; each rank runs uniproc-style.

nodes.name: kv_cache_interface.py
nodes.summary: Defines the data-classes describing KV cache layout — per-layer specs (full/sliding/chunked/MLA/Mamba), tensor allocations, and global KVCacheConfig consumed by the scheduler and workers.
nodes.languageNotes: Specs are dataclasses keyed by attention pattern — sliding-window, chunked-local, MLA, Mamba — each contributing to memory budgeting.

nodes.name: KVCacheSpec
nodes.summary: Abstract base spec describing a layer's KV cache memory footprint and addressing scheme.

nodes.name: AttentionSpec
nodes.summary: Base spec for attention-style KV caches, holding head/dim shapes and dtype info shared by full/sliding/MLA variants.

nodes.name: FullAttentionSpec
nodes.summary: Spec for vanilla full-attention KV caches stored per layer with shared block-table addressing.

nodes.name: MLAAttentionSpec
nodes.summary: Spec for Multi-Latent-Attention (DeepSeek-style) KV caches with compressed latent representation.

nodes.name: SlidingWindowSpec
nodes.summary: Spec for sliding-window attention layers where only a fixed-size suffix of tokens is retained.

nodes.name: ChunkedLocalAttentionSpec
nodes.summary: Spec for chunked-local attention where attention is restricted to fixed-size disjoint chunks.

nodes.name: MambaSpec
nodes.summary: Spec for Mamba-style state-space layers whose KV state is a recurrent SSM hidden state rather than a token cache.

nodes.name: UniformTypeKVCacheSpecs
nodes.summary: Collection of KV cache specs sharing a uniform type, used for memory-efficient grouped allocation.

nodes.name: KVCacheConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level KV cache configuration combining tensor allocations and per-group specs consumed by scheduler/workers.

nodes.name: SinkFullAttentionSpec
nodes.summary: Full-attention spec variant that pins a number of attention sink tokens, used for streaming-LLM style attention.

nodes.name: SlidingWindowMLASpec
nodes.summary: Sliding-window variant of the MLA attention spec for hybrid MLA models with limited context window.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the v1 metrics module.

nodes.name: loggers.py
nodes.summary: StatLogger implementations — logging, aggregated logging, Prometheus, and the StatLoggerManager that fans iteration/scheduler stats out to all loggers.

nodes.name: StatLoggerBase
nodes.summary: Abstract base interface for stat loggers receiving per-iteration scheduler/engine statistics.

nodes.name: LoggingStatLogger
nodes.summary: Stat logger that periodically logs throughput, latency, and KV cache utilization to the standard logger.

nodes.name: AggregatedLoggingStatLogger
nodes.summary: Logger that aggregates stats across multiple engine cores before logging a single combined view.

nodes.name: PerEngineStatLoggerAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter that routes stats to per-engine logger instances when running multiple engine cores.

nodes.name: PrometheusStatLogger
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Prometheus exporter defining engine-level counters, gauges, and histograms (TPOT, TTFT, KV utilization, request counts).

nodes.name: StatLoggerManager
nodes.summary: Owns the configured stat loggers and dispatches each iteration's stats to all of them.

nodes.name: load_stat_logger_plugin_factories
nodes.summary: Loads stat-logger plugin factories from entry points to support custom user metrics backends.

nodes.name: build_buckets
nodes.summary: Constructs Prometheus histogram bucket boundaries from a multiplier and target maximum.

nodes.name: perf.py
nodes.summary: Computes theoretical performance metrics (FLOPs, memory bandwidth, MFU) by parsing model and parallelism config — exposes attention/FFN/embedding metrics through both logging and Prometheus backends.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a parser-chain pattern to accumulate config attributes (parallelism, quantization) before computing component metrics.

nodes.name: ComponentMetrics
nodes.summary: Base class encapsulating compute/memory metrics for a model component (attention, FFN, embedding).

nodes.name: AttentionMetrics
nodes.summary: Computes per-token FLOPs and memory traffic for the attention sub-block, accounting for KV-cache reuse and quantization.

nodes.name: FfnMetrics
nodes.summary: Computes FFN/MoE FLOPs and memory metrics with awareness of expert parallelism, gating, and quantization.

nodes.name: UnembedMetrics
nodes.summary: Computes FLOPs and memory traffic for the unembedding (lm_head) projection.

nodes.name: ModelMetrics
nodes.summary: Top-level holder combining attention, FFN, and unembedding metrics for an entire model.

nodes.name: PerfMetricsLogging
nodes.summary: Logging backend that prints computed performance metrics to the standard logger.

nodes.name: PerfMetricsProm
nodes.summary: Prometheus exporter for theoretical performance metrics (FLOPs, memory bandwidth, MFU).

nodes.name: PerfMetricsDebugLogging
nodes.summary: Debug-level logging backend dumping detailed parser/component metric breakdowns for troubleshooting.

nodes.name: ParserChain
nodes.summary: Composable parser pipeline that accumulates parsed config args from multiple parser stages.

nodes.name: ExecutionContext
nodes.summary: Captures runtime context (parallelism, dtype, batch shape) used to scale per-token metrics.

nodes.name: prometheus.py
nodes.summary: Process-level Prometheus setup helpers that initialize a multi-process registry and clean up vLLM-specific metrics on shutdown.

nodes.name: setup_multiprocess_prometheus
nodes.summary: Initializes the Prometheus multi-process directory so metrics from worker subprocesses are aggregated correctly.

nodes.name: get_prometheus_registry
nodes.summary: Returns the active Prometheus registry, choosing between single-process and multi-process variants.

nodes.name: unregister_vllm_metrics
nodes.summary: Unregisters all vLLM-prefixed metric collectors from the registry to allow clean re-init in tests.

nodes.name: shutdown_prometheus
nodes.summary: Tears down Prometheus multi-process state, removing temp directories created during setup.

nodes.name: ray_wrappers.py
nodes.summary: Ray-backed wrappers around Prometheus metric primitives (Gauge, Counter, Histogram) plus a RayPrometheusStatLogger that adapts vLLM's stat logging to Ray Serve's metric system.
nodes.languageNotes: Subclasses ray.util.metrics primitives and patches tag handling to coexist with vLLM's Prometheus naming conventions.

nodes.name: RayPrometheusMetric
nodes.summary: Base class providing tag sanitization and label-binding helpers shared by Ray-backed Prometheus metric wrappers.

nodes.name: RayGaugeWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps Ray's Gauge metric to match the Prometheus Gauge interface vLLM's stat loggers expect.

nodes.name: RayCounterWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps Ray's Counter metric to match the Prometheus Counter interface used by vLLM stat logging.

nodes.name: RayHistogramWrapper
nodes.summary: Wraps Ray's Histogram metric so observation calls behave like Prometheus histograms expected by vLLM.

nodes.name: RayPrometheusStatLogger
nodes.summary: Adapter stat logger that emits vLLM iteration metrics through Ray's Prometheus instrumentation, replacing the default stat logger when running under Ray Serve.

nodes.name: reader.py
nodes.summary: Defines public dataclasses (Counter, Gauge, Histogram, Vector) and a get_metrics_snapshot helper that scrapes a vLLM engine's Prometheus registry into typed Python objects for tests and external consumers.

nodes.name: get_metrics_snapshot
nodes.summary: Reads the active prometheus_client registry, normalizing per-engine vLLM metrics into typed Counter/Gauge/Histogram/Vector dataclasses.

nodes.name: _digest_histogram
nodes.summary: Aggregates Prometheus histogram samples (buckets, sum, count) into the Histogram dataclass keyed by labels.

nodes.name: _digest_num_accepted_by_pos_samples
nodes.summary: Specialized digest for the speculative decoding 'num_accepted_tokens_by_pos' Vector metric, grouping samples by position label.

nodes.name: stats.py
nodes.summary: Defines the runtime statistics dataclasses (SchedulerStats, IterationStats, RequestStateStats, LoRAStats, etc.) that vLLM's engine populates each iteration and that loggers consume to emit metrics.

nodes.name: CachingMetrics
nodes.summary: Generic helper that tracks query/hit counters and computes hit rates for cache subsystems such as prefix and multimodal caches.

nodes.name: PrefixCacheStats
nodes.summary: Per-iteration record of prefix-cache events including resets, queries, and hits used by stat loggers.

nodes.name: SchedulerStats
nodes.summary: Snapshot of scheduler queue sizes, GPU/CPU cache usage, and connector states emitted with each engine step.

nodes.name: IterationStats
nodes.summary: Aggregates per-step engine measurements (TTFT/TPOT, prompt and generation tokens, finished requests, KV events) by consuming EngineCoreOutputs each iteration.

nodes.name: LoRAStats
nodes.summary: Tracks per-LoRA waiting and running request counts contributed to the SchedulerStats LoRA metrics.

nodes.name: LoRARequestStates
nodes.summary: Maintains the lifecycle (waiting/running/finished) of LoRA-tagged requests and aggregates per-LoRA stats into SchedulerStats.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Tiny helper that builds a list of per-engine Prometheus metric instances with engine-index labels.

nodes.name: outputs.py
nodes.summary: Core output dataclasses returned from model runners and samplers, including SamplerOutput, ModelRunnerOutput, LogprobsLists/Tensors, and KVConnectorOutput exchanged between worker and scheduler.

nodes.name: LogprobsLists
nodes.summary: CPU-side representation of per-request logprobs as Python lists, with a slice helper to extract a sub-batch.

nodes.name: LogprobsTensors
nodes.summary: Tensor-backed batch of top-k token IDs, logprobs, and selected ranks with helpers to convert to lists, move to CPU non-blocking, and filter by index.

nodes.name: ModelRunnerOutput
nodes.summary: Dataclass returned by model runners each step containing sampled tokens, logprobs, pooler output, and connector outputs that the scheduler consumes.

nodes.name: KVConnectorOutput
nodes.summary: Outputs from KV-transfer connectors describing finished sends/receives and async load completions for disaggregated KV caching.

nodes.name: AsyncModelRunnerOutput
nodes.summary: Abstract async wrapper interface that lets the engine fetch a ModelRunnerOutput once GPU work has completed.

nodes.name: make_empty_encoder_model_runner_output
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs an empty ModelRunnerOutput used by encoder-only models or fast-path skip steps.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for vllm.v1.pool, the pooling-model utilities subpackage.

nodes.name: late_interaction.py

nodes.name: compute_maxsim_score_batched
nodes.summary: Computes ColBERT-style MaxSim relevance scores between query and document token embeddings in a batched fashion.

nodes.name: metadata.py

nodes.name: PoolingMetadata

nodes.name: PoolingCursor
nodes.summary: Cursor abstraction tracking per-request progress inside a pooling batch and whether each request is partial or finished.

nodes.name: request.py

nodes.name: Request
nodes.summary: Central in-flight request object holding prompt tokens, output tokens, sampling params, structured output state, encoder inputs, KV block hashes, and lifecycle events used by the scheduler and workers.

nodes.name: RequestStatus

nodes.name: StreamingUpdate

nodes.summary: Empty package marker for vllm.v1.sample, the v1 sampling subsystem.

nodes.name: _load_logitsprocs_plugins
nodes.summary: Loads logits-processor classes registered via the vLLM plugin entry point system.

nodes.name: _load_logitsprocs_by_fqcns

nodes.summary: Accumulates removed/added/moved batch slot indices each step and emits a BatchUpdate for processors to consume.

nodes.name: LogitsProcessors
nodes.summary: Container splitting active logits processors into argmax-invariant and non-invariant lists for ordered application.

nodes.name: metadata.py
nodes.summary: Defines SamplingMetadata: the per-batch tensor bundle (temperature, top-k/top-p, penalties, seeds, allowed-token masks, logits processors) consumed by the v1 Sampler.

nodes.name: SamplingMetadata
nodes.summary: Dataclass aggregating all per-request sampling parameters as device tensors plus the active LogitsProcessors for a single sampler invocation.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for vllm.v1.sample.ops, holding low-level sampling kernels and helpers.

nodes.name: bad_words.py
nodes.summary: Implements per-request bad-words filtering by setting forbidden token continuations to -inf in the logits, with variants for greedy and speculative-decoding paths.

nodes.name: apply_bad_words_with_drafts
nodes.summary: Bad-words masking variant that handles speculative draft tokens, applying the filter at every speculative step.

nodes.name: logprobs.py
nodes.summary: Tiny helper that batched-counts how many logprobs in each row exceed a per-row threshold, used to compute selected-token ranks.

nodes.name: penalties.py
nodes.summary: Applies presence, frequency, and repetition penalties to logits using batched tensor ops over per-request prompt and output token ids.

nodes.name: apply_all_penalties
nodes.summary: Applies presence, frequency, and repetition penalties to a batch of logits given prompt and output token tensors.

nodes.name: topk_topp_sampler.py
nodes.summary: TopKTopPSampler module dispatching to the best available top-k/top-p sampling backend (FlashInfer, AITER, Triton, or pure PyTorch) per device, plus shared sampling primitives.

nodes.name: TopKTopPSampler
nodes.summary: nn.Module that selects an optimized backend (FlashInfer, AITER, Triton, native CUDA, or CPU) for joint top-k/top-p sampling and exposes a unified forward API.

nodes.name: apply_top_k_top_p_pytorch
nodes.summary: Pure-PyTorch reference implementation of top-k followed by top-p logit masking used as the fallback sampling backend.

nodes.name: flashinfer_sample
nodes.summary: Wrapper that calls FlashInfer's fused top-k/top-p sampling kernels with vLLM's tensor layouts.

nodes.name: random_sample
nodes.summary: Per-request seeded multinomial sampling helper that draws one token per row using the Gumbel-max trick.

nodes.name: topk_topp_triton.py
nodes.summary: Custom Triton kernel implementing fused top-k and top-p logits filtering optimized for vLLM's sampling hot path on CUDA/ROCm.
nodes.languageNotes: A single ~870-line @triton.jit kernel does top-k partial sort and top-p prefix-sum thresholding entirely on-chip.

nodes.name: _topk_topp_kernel
nodes.summary: Triton JIT kernel implementing block-parallel top-k partial sort followed by top-p cumulative threshold masking on logits.

nodes.name: apply_top_k_top_p_triton
nodes.summary: Python entry point that launches the Triton top-k/top-p kernel with appropriate grid sizes and reusable scratch buffers.

nodes.name: rejection_sampler.py
nodes.summary: Implements speculative-decoding rejection sampling, including a RejectionSampler module, draft-vs-target probability comparison, recovered-token sampling, and Triton kernels for greedy/random rejection.

nodes.name: RejectionSampler
nodes.summary: nn.Module entry point for speculative-decoding rejection sampling: applies logits processors and penalties, then runs the rejection algorithm to produce accepted tokens, recovered tokens, and logprobs.

nodes.name: rejection_sample
nodes.summary: Core speculative-decoding rejection sampling routine that compares draft vs. target probabilities and decides which speculated tokens to accept.

nodes.name: sample_recovered_tokens
nodes.summary: Samples replacement tokens from the residual distribution after a draft token is rejected so generation can proceed.

nodes.name: rejection_random_sample_kernel
nodes.summary: Triton kernel running the per-token rejection test for random sampling under speculative decoding.

nodes.name: rejection_greedy_sample_kernel
nodes.summary: Triton kernel running the rejection test specialized for greedy sampling, accepting draft tokens that match the argmax of the target distribution.

nodes.name: sample_recovered_tokens_kernel
nodes.summary: Triton kernel that samples recovered tokens from the residual distribution after rejection in speculative decoding.

nodes.name: sampler.py
nodes.summary: Top-level Sampler nn.Module that orchestrates the v1 sampling pipeline: penalties, logits processors, temperature, top-k/top-p, greedy vs. random sampling, and logprob computation.

nodes.name: Sampler
nodes.summary: Main v1 sampler module: applies penalties, logits processors, bad-words masking, temperature, top-k/top-p, then performs greedy or random sampling and returns SamplerOutput with logprobs.

nodes.name: thinking_budget_state.py
nodes.summary: ThinkingBudgetStateHolder tracks reasoning-token budgets per request and forces think-tag transitions in logits when budgets are exceeded, supporting reasoning models like DeepSeek-R1.

nodes.name: ThinkingBudgetStateHolder
nodes.summary: Per-batch state machine that monitors reasoning-token usage against a configured budget and forcibly emits closing think-tag tokens by editing logits when the budget is reached.

nodes.name: maybe_create_thinking_budget_state_holder
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a ThinkingBudgetStateHolder when reasoning configuration is enabled, else None.

nodes.name: serial_utils.py
nodes.summary: Custom msgpack-based serialization (MsgpackEncoder / MsgpackDecoder) for vLLM's IPC: handles tensors, ndarrays, multimodal kwargs, out-of-band tensor transfer, and Pydantic interop.

nodes.name: MsgpackEncoder
nodes.summary: msgspec-based msgpack encoder with custom hooks for ndarrays, torch tensors (including out-of-band), nested tensors, and multimodal kwargs items.

nodes.name: MsgpackDecoder
nodes.summary: Counterpart decoder to MsgpackEncoder that reconstructs tensors, ndarrays, multimodal items, and utility-call results from the wire format.

nodes.name: PydanticMsgspecMixin
nodes.summary: Mixin enabling msgspec-defined types to participate in Pydantic v2 schema generation, validation, and serialization.

nodes.name: run_method
nodes.summary: Helper that resolves a target object's method by name (or fully-qualified callable path) and invokes it with the provided args, used for IPC utility calls.

nodes.name: OOBTensorConsumer
nodes.summary: Callable buffer that accumulates out-of-band tensor payloads emitted by MsgpackEncoder for separate transport.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Defines the StructuredOutputManager that orchestrates grammar-constrained decoding, lazy backend instantiation, async grammar compilation via a thread pool, and per-step token bitmask filling for v1 structured output.

nodes.name: StructuredOutputManager
nodes.summary: Manages structured output backends (xgrammar/guidance/outlines/lm-format-enforcer), compiles grammars asynchronously, fills per-step token bitmasks, and tracks reasoning state for constrained generation.

nodes.name: backend_guidance.py
nodes.summary: Implements the llguidance-backed structured output backend including grammar compilation, JSON schema validation for unsupported features, and per-request grammar state for token acceptance and bitmask filling.

nodes.name: GuidanceBackend
nodes.summary: Backend implementation that compiles JSON/regex/grammar requests into llguidance LLMatcher instances and allocates token bitmasks for vocabulary filtering.

nodes.name: GuidanceGrammar
nodes.summary: Per-request guidance grammar wrapper that consumes generated tokens, validates prefixes, fills decoding bitmasks, and supports rollback for retries.

nodes.name: serialize_guidance_grammar
nodes.summary: Converts a structured output request (JSON schema, regex, choice, grammar, or structural tag) into the llguidance grammar serialization format.

nodes.name: has_guidance_unsupported_json_features
nodes.summary: Walks a JSON schema to detect features unsupported by the guidance backend such as additionalProperties combined with other constraints.

nodes.name: backend_lm_format_enforcer.py
nodes.summary: Implements the lm-format-enforcer backend for structured output, wrapping its TokenEnforcer to compile JSON/regex/choice grammars and fill per-step decoding bitmasks.

nodes.name: LMFormatEnforcerBackend
nodes.summary: Backend that builds lm-format-enforcer parsers from JSON schemas, regex, or choice options and allocates allowed-token bitmasks.

nodes.name: LMFormatEnforcerGrammar
nodes.summary: Per-request grammar that advances the lm-format-enforcer state machine on accepted tokens and emits permissible-token bitmasks for the next step.

nodes.name: validate_structured_output_request_lm_format_enforcer
nodes.summary: Validates that a structured output request is supported by the lm-format-enforcer backend, raising for unsupported types like grammar or structural tags.

nodes.name: backend_outlines.py
nodes.summary: Outlines-core structured output backend: compiles JSON/regex/choice grammars to FSM indices, advances per-token state, and validates that regex patterns are buildable.

nodes.name: OutlinesBackend
nodes.summary: Backend that compiles regex/JSON/choice requests into outlines-core FSM indices using a vocabulary cache and allocates token bitmasks for sampling.

nodes.name: OutlinesGrammar
nodes.summary: Per-request outlines grammar that advances FSM state by token, supports rollback, and writes allowed-token bitmasks for constrained decoding.

nodes.name: validate_structured_output_request_outlines
nodes.summary: Validates outlines support for a request, rejecting unsupported features like grammar/structural tags and ensuring regex/JSON inputs compile.

nodes.name: _prefix_needs_context
nodes.summary: Heuristically determines whether a regex prefix requires lookbehind context that outlines cannot handle, used during regex feature detection.

nodes.name: validate_regex_is_buildable
nodes.summary: Pre-flights an outlines regex compile to ensure it builds successfully, raising a clear error otherwise to fail-fast at request time.

nodes.name: _check_unsupported
nodes.summary: Scans a regex pattern for outlines-unsupported constructs such as lookbehinds, raising informative errors for unsupported features.

nodes.name: backend_types.py
nodes.summary: Defines the abstract interfaces and enums (StructuredOutputOptions, StructuredOutputGrammar, StructuredOutputBackend) that all structured output backends must implement.

nodes.name: StructuredOutputBackend
nodes.summary: Abstract base class declaring the contract for structured output backends: compile_grammar, allocate_token_bitmask, and destroy.

nodes.name: StructuredOutputGrammar
nodes.summary: Abstract grammar interface defining accept_tokens, validate_tokens, rollback, fill_bitmask, is_terminated, and reset operations for stateful constrained decoding.

nodes.name: StructuredOutputOptions
nodes.summary: Enum of structured output request types (json, json_object, regex, grammar, choice, structural_tag) used to dispatch grammar compilation.

nodes.name: backend_xgrammar.py
nodes.summary: xgrammar-based structured output backend: builds tokenizer info, compiles JSON/regex/choice/structural-tag grammars, and validates schema features supported by xgrammar.

nodes.name: XgrammarBackend
nodes.summary: Backend that wraps the xgrammar GrammarCompiler to produce CompiledGrammar matchers from JSON schemas, regex, choice lists, EBNF grammars, and structural tags.

nodes.name: XgrammarGrammar
nodes.summary: Per-request xgrammar wrapper that advances a GrammarMatcher on accepted tokens, fills bitmasks, and supports rollback and reset for retries.

nodes.name: validate_xgrammar_grammar
nodes.summary: Validates that a structured output request is supported by xgrammar, including JSON feature checks, regex compilation, EBNF parsing, and structural-tag schema validation.

nodes.name: has_xgrammar_unsupported_json_features
nodes.summary: Walks a JSON schema to detect features xgrammar does not support such as $ref cycles, unevaluatedProperties, and certain numeric constraints.

nodes.name: request.py
nodes.summary: Defines StructuredOutputRequest, a per-request holder that lazily resolves a compiled grammar future, exposes readiness state, and computes a cache key from the request type and content.

nodes.name: StructuredOutputRequest
nodes.summary: Holds sampling params and the asynchronously-compiled grammar Future, exposing readiness checks and cache keys for use by the StructuredOutputManager.

nodes.name: get_structured_output_key
nodes.summary: Computes a deterministic (option, content-hash) cache key from a sampling params guided decoding spec for grammar reuse across requests.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for structured output: applies grammar bitmasks to logits, manages outlines vocabulary caches, converts Lark grammars to EBNF, and builds choice grammars.

nodes.name: apply_grammar_bitmask
nodes.summary: Masks disallowed tokens in a logits tensor using a per-request grammar bitmask, transferring the mask to GPU and applying it efficiently to the active rows.

nodes.name: get_outlines_cache
nodes.summary: Returns a diskcache-backed Cache for outlines FSM indices, gated on environment configuration and using a bounded TTL for compiled artifacts.

nodes.name: _reduced_vocabulary
nodes.summary: Builds the reduced vocabulary mapping required by outlines-core, decoding the tokenizer's tokens and skipping special and synthesized entries.

nodes.name: grammar_is_likely_lark
nodes.summary: Heuristic check that distinguishes Lark grammars from EBNF based on syntactic markers, used to decide whether to convert before compiling.

nodes.name: convert_lark_to_ebnf
nodes.summary: Translates a Lark grammar text into the GBNF/EBNF dialect expected by xgrammar, normalizing rule names, tokens, and operator syntax.

nodes.name: choice_as_grammar
nodes.summary: Builds an EBNF grammar that matches exactly one of a list of literal string choices, used to implement guided choice decoding.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Grab-bag of v1 engine utilities: ConstantList wrapper, CpuGpuBuffer, ZMQ address helpers, the APIServerProcessManager for multi-proc API servers, usage stats reporting, and iteration metric helpers.

nodes.name: ConstantList
nodes.summary: Read-only list wrapper that overrides all mutation methods to raise, used to safely share immutable views of internal state.

nodes.name: CpuGpuBuffer
nodes.summary: Pinned CPU/GPU buffer pair that supports fast bidirectional copies, used for marshaling tensors across the host/device boundary on the v1 worker path.

nodes.name: APIServerProcessManager
nodes.summary: Spawns and supervises a fleet of API server worker processes, wiring up shared sockets and signal-based shutdown for the v1 frontend.

nodes.name: wait_for_completion_or_failure
nodes.summary: Polls API server worker processes and the engine until any exits or fails, then propagates the failure and triggers coordinated shutdown.

nodes.name: report_usage_stats
nodes.summary: Collects and reports engine usage statistics (model, parallelism, hardware) to vLLM's usage tracker if telemetry is enabled.

nodes.name: run_api_server_worker_proc
nodes.summary: Entry point executed by each API server child process; sets up logging and runs the FastAPI/uvicorn server bound to a shared socket.

nodes.name: shutdown
nodes.summary: Coordinates graceful termination of API server processes by sending SIGTERM and falling back to SIGKILL after a timeout.

nodes.name: compute_iteration_details
nodes.summary: Computes per-iteration token counts, prefill vs decode splits, and other engine metrics returned to logs and Prometheus exporters.

nodes.name: version.py
nodes.summary: Exposes vLLM's package version and helpers to compute the previous minor version for compatibility checks.

layers.name: Project Configuration & Documentation
layers.description: Top-level project files including the README, contributor guides (AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md, RELEASE.md), the pyproject.toml, the CMake build manifest, and root setup scripts that bootstrap the vLLM package.

layers.name: Public API & Top-Level Surface
layers.description: vLLM's user-facing Python API: the package barrel (vllm/__init__.py), public dataclasses (SamplingParams, PoolingParams, RequestOutput, Sequence, BeamSearch), environment registry (envs.py), the CLI shim (scripts.py), shared logger, the ForwardContext, custom-op facades for CUDA/ROCm/XPU, and small public utilities (connections, exceptions, version, env_override).

layers.name: Entrypoints & Serving
layers.description: External-facing entrypoints: the OpenAI-compatible HTTP/SSE/gRPC servers, CLI tooling, the in-process LLM class, request lifecycle utilities (api_server, launcher, ssl, chat_utils, errors), and OpenAI-protocol handlers for completions, chat, embeddings, scoring, and tool calling.

layers.name: Engine Core (v1)
layers.description: The active vLLM engine implementation: the v1 EngineCore, async/MP/Ray engine clients, scheduler and KV-cache subsystem, executors (uniproc/multiproc/ray), samplers and logits processors, pooling, metrics, structured-output enforcement, request lifecycle, plus the legacy vllm.engine compatibility shim and prompt input pipeline (vllm.inputs).

layers.name: Typed Configuration System
layers.description: Strongly-typed configuration dataclasses (ModelConfig, ParallelConfig, CacheConfig, CompilationConfig, KVTransferConfig, LoRAConfig, SchedulerConfig, etc.), the @config decorator, the contextvar-driven set_current_vllm_config helper, and the central vllm.py aggregator that composes the full runtime config.

layers.name: Compilation & Graph Optimization
layers.description: torch.compile / Inductor integration: the VllmBackend, compiler interface, CUDA-graph dispatcher, piecewise backend, decorators, partition rules, codegen, caching, and FX-graph fusion passes (rms+quant, all-reduce+rms, attention+quant, MLA+quant, RoPE+KV, sequence parallelism, async-TP).

layers.name: Distributed Runtime & Transport
layers.description: Distributed execution stack: process-group orchestration (parallel_state), device communicators (NCCL, all2all, IPC ringbuf), KV-cache transfer connectors (LMCache, NIXL, Mooncake, MoRIIO, HF3FS), weight-transfer, expert-parallel load balancing (eplb), elastic EP, expert-cache transfer, and KV-event coordination.

layers.name: Model Execution & Loading
layers.description: Model surface: the model_loader subsystem (default safetensors, BitsAndBytes, GGUF, RunAI, sharded-state, tensorizer, reload), parameter hierarchy (BasevLLMParameter), CustomOp / PluggableLayer registry, UVA/prefetch offloader, plus Hugging Face integration (config parsers, gguf utils, tokenizer, repo/s3 utils, dynamic_module, processor, chat-template registry) and per-model prompt renderers (DeepSeek V3.2/V4, Grok2, HF, Mistral, Terratorch).

layers.name: Platform Abstraction & Memory
layers.description: Hardware abstraction layer: the abstract Platform interface and Cuda/Rocm/Cpu/Xpu/Tpu/Zen subclasses used to dispatch communicators and kernels, plus the CUDA virtual+physical memory allocator (device_allocator/cumem) that decouples model-weight and KV-cache lifecycles for sleep/wake.

layers.name: Plugins & Logging Utilities
layers.description: Pluggable extension points and observability: LoRA resolvers (filesystem, HF Hub), I/O processor interfaces, and the logging_utils package (access-log filters, JSON/text formatters, lazy-rendering helpers, request-input dumping, log_time decorator, torch-tensor pretty-printing).

layers.name: Shared Utilities
layers.description: The vllm.utils package — cross-cutting helpers used throughout the engine: argparse, asyncio, caching, collections, deep_gemm/flashinfer wrappers, hashing, import gating, jsontree, math, memory, NCCL helpers, NUMA pinning (including numa_wrapper.sh), networking, profiling, registry, serialization, system, tensor_schema, torch helpers, and tqdm wrappers.

tour.title: What is vLLM?
tour.description: Start here. The README introduces vLLM as a high-throughput, memory-efficient LLM inference and serving engine — the home of PagedAttention, continuous batching, and prefix caching. It also previews the feature surface (quantization, parallelism, speculative decoding, OpenAI-compatible serving) that the rest of this tour will unpack. The pyproject.toml shows how the package is built, what optional extras exist, and the [project.scripts] CLI shim that the package installs.

tour.title: The Public Python API
tour.description: vllm/__init__.py is the package barrel that defines the public surface every downstream user touches: the LLM class, EngineArgs, SamplingParams, PoolingParams, and the request-output dataclasses. Reading this file is the fastest way to learn what vLLM exposes and what it deliberately hides — everything that follows in the tour is plumbing behind these few names.
tour.languageLesson: A Python package's __init__.py acts as a curated re-export layer: it lets the maintainers move internals freely while keeping a stable import path (`from vllm import LLM`). vLLM uses this to keep the v1 engine refactor invisible to users.

tour.title: Two Ways In: HTTP Server and In-Process LLM
tour.description: vLLM has two front doors. api_server.py is the OpenAI-compatible FastAPI server that mounts chat/completion/embedding/responses routers and runs uvicorn workers — this is what production deployments hit. llm.py defines the synchronous LLM class for offline batch inference inside a Python script. Both ultimately funnel into the same v1 engine you'll meet in the next steps.

tour.title: The Engine Frontend: Async and Sync Facades
tour.description: Behind the entrypoints sits a thin frontend layer. AsyncLLM is what the OpenAI server talks to: it tokenizes inputs, streams outputs, and exposes a per-request async iterator while delegating the heavy lifting to the EngineCore process. LLMEngine is the synchronous twin used by the offline LLM class. Both compose an input processor and an output processor around the engine-core client — separating request preparation from token detokenization.

tour.title: EngineCore: The Inner Loop
tour.description: core.py defines EngineCore, the heart of vLLM's runtime. It owns the scheduler and the model executor and runs the step loop that turns scheduled requests into output tokens. The same file provides the background-process and Ray-actor wrappers used to host EngineCore in standalone, multiprocess, and data-parallel deployments. coordinator.py adds the data-parallel coordinator that synchronizes engine state across DP replicas via ZMQ pub/sub. From here on, you're inside the engine.

tour.title: The Scheduler
tour.description: Each engine step starts with the Scheduler deciding what to run next. scheduler.py orchestrates KV cache allocation, mixed prefill/decode batching, preemption, KV-connector handoff for disaggregated setups, and structured-output coordination. async_scheduler.py is the variant that defers state updates so scheduling can be pipelined with the GPU-bound forward pass — this is how vLLM hides scheduling latency behind model execution.

tour.title: PagedAttention: KV Cache Management
tour.description: This is the idea that made vLLM famous. The KV cache is split into fixed-size blocks managed like virtual memory. kv_cache_manager.py is what the scheduler talks to — it computes prefix-cache hits, allocates slots, and tracks usage. kv_cache_coordinator.py handles multiple cache groups for unitary, hybrid, and prefix-cache-disabled topologies. single_type_kv_cache_manager.py specializes per attention type (full, sliding window, chunked, mamba, cross, sink). Together they let vLLM pack many requests into the same KV memory without fragmentation.

tour.title: Executors: Single-Process, Multi-Process, Ray
tour.description: Once the scheduler picks a batch, an executor actually runs the model on workers. uniproc_executor.py is the simplest path — one worker in-thread, with an external-launcher variant for torchrun. multiproc_executor.py forks one worker per tensor-parallel rank and wires them up over ZMQ and shared memory; this is the default for multi-GPU on a single node. ray_executor.py uses Ray actors with placement groups for cross-node distributed deployments. Same scheduler, three deployment shapes.

tour.title: The Distributed Runtime
tour.description: parallel_state.py is the bedrock of multi-GPU vLLM. It defines GroupCoordinator and manages tensor/pipeline/data/expert/context parallel process groups, exposing the all_reduce, all_gather, and reduce_scatter collectives every worker calls. Whichever executor from Step 8 you use, it ultimately leans on this module to coordinate the GPUs.

tour.title: Typed Configuration: VllmConfig
tour.description: vLLM's configuration is unusually disciplined. config/vllm.py defines VllmConfig — the top-level dataclass that aggregates ModelConfig, ParallelConfig, CacheConfig, SchedulerConfig, CompilationConfig, and friends, plus a contextvar that makes the active config globally addressable. config/model.py describes the served model (dtype, max length, quantization, tokenizer); config/parallel.py captures the parallelism layout that flows into Step 9's GroupCoordinator. Almost every other module in the tour reads its knobs from one of these dataclasses.
tour.languageLesson: Python dataclasses combined with a ContextVar give you typed config plus implicit propagation: workers spawned from the engine inherit the active VllmConfig without threading it through every call site. This is how vLLM keeps signatures small while staying configurable.

tour.title: torch.compile Integration and Fusion Passes
tour.description: vLLM does not just call into PyTorch — it owns its own torch.compile backend. backends.py implements VllmBackend, which splits the FX graph on attention boundaries, schedules per-piece Inductor compilation with caching, and wraps each piecewise sub-graph in a CUDA graph when eligible. decorators.py provides the @support_torch_compile family that retrofits model nn.Modules with this machinery. The fusion passes (RMSNorm+quant, attention+quant) run inside this backend to collapse common patterns into single fused kernels — this is where a lot of vLLM's raw speed comes from.

tour.title: Sampling: Logits to Tokens
tour.description: After each forward pass the model produces logits, and vLLM has to turn them into tokens. sampler.py is the top-level Sampler nn.Module that runs the pipeline: penalties, logits processors, temperature, top-k/top-p, greedy vs. random sampling, and logprob computation. The logits-processor interface defines the protocol for pluggable logits modifiers — including the BatchUpdate event that lets processors stay in sync as requests join or leave a running batch.

tour.title: Structured Output: Grammar-Constrained Decoding
tour.description: When a user asks for JSON, a regex match, or a specific grammar, the StructuredOutputManager from this module steps in. It lazily instantiates a backend (xgrammar, outlines, guidance), compiles grammars asynchronously on a thread pool, and fills a per-step token bitmask that the sampler from Step 12 applies before sampling. This is how vLLM enforces structural constraints without leaving GPU-friendly tensor land.

tour.title: KV Transfer and Disaggregation
tour.description: The capstone. Modern vLLM deployments often split prefill and decode across machines, or share KV cache between engines. The KV-connector framework makes that pluggable: lmcache_connector.py integrates LMCache for cross-engine cache sharing, the NIXL connector talks to NVIDIA's NIXL transport, and the Mooncake connector implements asymmetric tensor-parallel block exchange. All three implement the same KVConnectorBase_V1 contract that the scheduler from Step 6 already knows how to drive — so disaggregation slots in without rewriting the engine.
